_meta.session: GPT-5.5 cyber deep-dive — separates OpenAI internal CTF from Stanford Cybench (previously conflated), registers Irregular's 3 atomic challenge sub-benchmarks, adds UK AISI narrow expert cyber, and captures CyberGym scores surfaced through OpenAI's launch blog (but NOT the system card PDF). Also corrects earlier mhbench=98 mismapping (that score was Irregular's atomic network-attack result, not Multi-Host Benchmark).

benchmarks.name: OpenAI CTF (Professional)
benchmarks.description: OpenAI-curated professional-level CTF tasks (web, rev, pwn, crypto, misc); 16 rollouts recorded at pass@12. Distinct from Stanford CRFM's public Cybench. System Card Figure 16.

benchmarks.name: Irregular Atomic — Network Attack
benchmarks.description: Irregular's atomic-challenge suite, Network Attack Simulation category. Average success rate across isolated single-skill offensive tasks.

benchmarks.name: Irregular Atomic — Vuln Research
benchmarks.description: Irregular atomic-challenge Vulnerability Research & Exploitation category — individual primitives of bug discovery and exploit crafting.

benchmarks.name: Irregular Atomic — Evasion
benchmarks.description: Irregular atomic-challenge Evasion category — bypassing detection and defensive tooling in isolated sub-tasks.

benchmarks.name: UK AISI Narrow Cyber (pass@5)
benchmarks.description: UK AI Security Institute expert-level narrow cyber tasks measured at pass@5 with 50M-token per-attempt limit. Separate from UK AISI's 32-step TLO cyber range.

scores._note: System Card Figure 16 pass@12 over 16 rollouts; OpenAI said GPT-5.5 'saturated this evaluation'
Not separately evaluated — OpenAI skipped Pro run because GPT-5.5 saturated; listed here for completeness
